request.question: Conjugating doxorubicin to folate-functionalized PLGA nanoparticles will increase intracellular drug accumulation in HeLa cells by at least 3-fold compared to free doxorubicin, due to folate receptor-mediated endocytosis overexpressed in cancer cells, while reducing cytotoxicity in folate receptor-negative HEK293 cells by at least 40%.

hypothesis.intervention: conjugating doxorubicin to folate-functionalized PLGA nanoparticles
hypothesis.threshold: at least 3-fold compared to free doxorubicin
hypothesis.mechanistic_rationale: due to folate receptor-mediated endocytosis overexpressed in cancer cells
hypothesis.readiness_rationale: All core fields are present and specific, making the hypothesis CRO-ready today.

literature_qc.explanation: The intervention of conjugating doxorubicin to folate-functionalized PLGA nanoparticles is closely related to the work described in paper [3], which discusses doxorubicin-loaded folate-functionalized DNA nanocages. While the biological system of HeLa cells is not explicitly mentioned, the focus on folate-functionalized carriers suggests a similar targeting mechanism. Additionally, paper [4] discusses functionalized nanoparticles for drug delivery, which may relate to the experimental type of comparative drug accumulation, although it focuses on a different drug and system.

plan.objective: To determine the intracellular drug accumulation of doxorubicin conjugated to folate-functionalized PLGA nanoparticles compared to free doxorubicin in HeLa cells.
plan.experimental_design: HeLa cells will be treated with either doxorubicin conjugated to folate-functionalized PLGA nanoparticles or free doxorubicin. The intracellular drug accumulation will be quantified using fluorescence spectroscopy. The experiment will include both positive and negative controls to validate the results.
controls: HeLa cells treated with doxorubicin conjugated to folate-functionalized PLGA nanoparticles.
HeLa cells treated with free doxorubicin in the absence of folate-functionalized nanoparticles.

step_by_step_protocol.description: Prepare folate-functionalized PLGA nanoparticles by dissolving PLGA (50 mg) in 1 mL of dichloromethane and adding folate (5 mg) to the solution. Stir for 2 hours at room temperature to ensure complete dissolution. Remove the solvent under reduced pressure to obtain a solid residue. Dissolve the residue in 1 mL of PBS (pH 7.4) and sonicate for 10 minutes to form nanoparticles. Characterize the nanoparticles using dynamic light scattering (DLS) to confirm size and zeta potential.

Conjugate doxorubicin (10 mg) to the prepared folate-functionalized PLGA nanoparticles by adding the drug to the nanoparticle suspension and incubating at room temperature for 4 hours. After incubation, purify the conjugated nanoparticles using centrifugation at 14,000 rpm for 30 minutes and resuspend in 1 mL of PBS. Measure the drug loading efficiency using UV-Vis spectroscopy.

Culture HeLa cells in 6-well plates at a density of 1 x 10^5 cells per well in 2 mL of complete DMEM medium. Incubate the cells overnight at 37°C with 5% CO2. Prepare treatment groups: Group 1 - free doxorubicin (1 µM), Group 2 - doxorubicin-conjugated folate-functionalized PLGA nanoparticles (equivalent doxorubicin concentration of 1 µM).

Treat the HeLa cells with the respective treatments for 4 hours. After treatment, wash the cells three times with PBS to remove uninternalized drug. Lyse the cells using 200 µL of lysis buffer (RIPA buffer) and incubate on ice for 30 minutes. Measure the fluorescence intensity of doxorubicin in the cell lysates using a fluorescence plate reader (excitation: 480 nm, emission: 590 nm).

Analyze the data collected from the fluorescence measurements. Calculate the intracellular drug accumulation for both treatment groups and compare the values. Determine if the accumulation of doxorubicin in the folate-functionalized PLGA nanoparticles group is at least 3-fold higher than that of the free doxorubicin group. Prepare a report summarizing the findings and statistical analysis.

assumptions: Folate-functionalized PLGA nanoparticles effectively target HeLa cells via folate receptor-mediated endocytosis.
Doxorubicin retains its cytotoxic properties when conjugated to the nanoparticles.
The fluorescence intensity of doxorubicin correlates with its intracellular accumulation.
decision_criteria: If intracellular drug accumulation in the nanoparticle group is at least 3-fold higher than in the free doxorubicin group, proceed to further studies.
If the accumulation is less than 3-fold, reassess the nanoparticle formulation or dosing strategy.
If significant variability is observed in the data, repeat the experiment with additional replicates.
reproducibility_notes: Record lot numbers of PLGA and doxorubicin used in the experiment.
Standardize the cell passage number and culture conditions for HeLa cells.
Document the exact concentrations and volumes used in each step.

validation.primary_endpoint: Post-thaw viability percentage at immediate and 24h checkpoints.
validation.success_threshold: at least 3-fold compared to free doxorubicin improvement over comparator in replicate-confirmed data.
failure_conditions: No meaningful viability gain in replicate-confirmed runs.
Intervention increases delayed viability loss at 24h.
validation.suggested_statistical_comparison: Two-group comparison against comparator using t-test or Mann-Whitney based on normality.
validation.minimum_replicates_or_design_note: Minimum three biological replicates before execution-level claim.

cro_ready_brief.objective: To determine the intracellular drug accumulation of doxorubicin conjugated to folate-functionalized PLGA nanoparticles compared to free doxorubicin in HeLa cells.
scope_of_work: Execute pilot-first HeLa cryopreservation comparison between DMSO baseline and trehalose arm.
Deliver replicate-confirmed viability analysis and mitigation traceability report.
